query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of my findings:

## Bottom line: No extension beyond July 31, 2026 has been announced (as of July 3, 2026)

**Part 1 — Extension status:** I found **no cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June or early July 2026** (via TASS, Reuters, government.ru, or Interfax) extending Russia's gasoline export ban past **July 31, 2026**. The current ban — covering both non-producers and direct producers — was set by a **decree signed April 2, 2026** (government.ru news/58316). Notably, the government DID announce a separate **jet fuel export ban through end-November 2026 on June 1**, and Putin admitted a fuel deficit on June 29 — both raise the odds of a gasoline extension, but neither constitutes one.

**Part 2 — Drone strikes & shortages (late June 2026):**
- June 18: largest-ever drone attack on Moscow hit a Gazprom Neft refinery ~15 km south of the city
- June 28: Slavyansk-na-Kubani refinery (Krasnodar, ~4M t/yr) set ablaze; Yaroslavl also struck
- June 29: Putin admitted a "certain deficit" of fuel on state TV
- Crimea declared a state of emergency and suspended gasoline sales; Irkutsk (Siberia) imposed 50 L/vehicle/day rationing; restrictions in dozens of regions; Russia moved to import fuel

**Part 3 — Historical timing of extension announcements:** Russia consistently announces extensions only **~4–5 days before the deadline**:
- Dec 31, 2025 deadline → decree to Feb 28, 2026 announced **Dec 27, 2025 (~4 days prior)**
- Sept 30, 2025 deadline → Novak signaled extension **Sept 25, 2025 (~5 days prior)**

This pattern implies any July 2026 extension, if it materializes, would most likely be formally announced in the **final days of July (~July 27–31)**.

Note: The AskNews tools were unavailable (credit limit reached), so findings are drawn from web search, TASS, The Moscow Times, Bloomberg, Fortune, CNBC, CBC, and Global Trade Alert.

sources.snippet: Fuel restrictions reported in dozens of Russian regions; Russia, a top oil exporter, moved to import fuel amid shortages.

key_facts: No extension of Russia's gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 has been officially announced as of July 3, 2026 (no TASS/Reuters/government.ru decree found).
The current gasoline export ban (non-producers AND producers) runs until July 31, 2026, per a decree signed April 2, 2026 (government.ru news/58316).
The ban was first re-imposed on non-producers effective April 1, 2026, then extended to direct producers on April 2, 2026.
On June 1, 2026, Russia's government announced a separate JET FUEL export ban through end-November 2026 (government.ru news/58885), showing active fuel-export tightening.
Putin publicly admitted a fuel 'deficit' in a June 29, 2026 state-TV interview - his clearest such admission.
June 18, 2026: largest-ever Ukrainian drone attack on Moscow struck a Gazprom Neft refinery ~15 km south of the city.
June 28, 2026: drones set fire to the Slavyansk-na-Kubani refinery (Krasnodar, ~4 million tons/yr crude capacity), killing 1; Yaroslavl region also hit.
Crimea declared a state of emergency in June 2026 and suspended civilian gasoline sales - worst energy crisis there since annexation.
Siberia (Irkutsk region) imposed rationing of max 50 liters/vehicle/day at state Rosneft stations; fuel restrictions reported in dozens of regions.
Historical pattern: extensions announced ~4-5 days before deadline (Dec 31, 2025 deadline -> Dec 27, 2025 decree to Feb 28, 2026; Sept 30, 2025 deadline -> Novak signaled Sept 25, 2025).
